config.seed_text: A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: The code was all but useless for understanding it; instead of solving problems using simple equations or trying to learn new techniques through experimentation, people simply typed out questions on paper onto computers while playing with words they had never used before—like "What's your favorite color?" when you read someone else's response into an email box (you could easily pick from these answers even if others didn't notice.)
records.output_text: Then those who knew something about this question would try to get back at them by answering other clues that appeared just under their eyes during each sentence themselves. This is where I came up with A Thousand Words Of The World . As soon we learned how hard math works today, our own brains developed strategies which allowed us apply physics only because science told us so many useful things couldn' t be applied without doing some homework! One such strategy involved reading sentences like one might imagine being asked ' What do you think makes sense? Would anyone object?' Here lie parts 1 thru 11… That explains why every person knows what happens once he leaves his cell room... You can see my mind whirring inside me right now!! My brain has spent years writing rules here no matter whether she wants to understand anything beyond first

TEXT:
A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware. The code was all but useless for understanding it; instead of solving problems using simple equations or trying to learn new techniques through experimentation, people simply typed out questions on paper onto computers while playing with words they had never used before—like "What's your favorite color?" when you read someone else's response into an email box (you could easily pick from these answers even if others didn't notice.)

ORIGINAL TEXT:
A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.

REFLECTION:
The code was all but useless for understanding it; instead of solving problems using simple equations or trying to learn new techniques through experimentation, people simply typed out questions on paper onto computers while playing with words they had never used before—like "What's your favorite color?" when you read someone else's response into an email box (you could easily pick from these answers even if others didn't notice.) Then those who knew something about this question would try to get back at them by answering other clues that appeared just under their eyes during each sentence themselves. This is where I came up with A Thousand Words Of The World . As soon we learned how hard math works today, our own brains developed strategies which allowed us apply physics only because science told us so many useful things couldn' t be applied without doing some homework! One such strategy involved reading sentences like one might imagine being asked ' What do you think makes sense? Would anyone object?' Here lie parts 1 thru 11… That explains why every person knows what happens once he leaves his cell room... You can see my mind whirring inside me right now!! My brain has spent years writing rules here no matter whether she wants to understand anything beyond first
